summary:
With an overall rating of 85/100, Blueberry Futures enters the market as a new futures-focused arm of the Blueberry brand, offering two one-step challenges with fast execution and clear trading rules. You can trade deep-liquidity CME markets covering forex, commodities, and indices on the prop firm's BlackArrow futures trading platform.

Blueberry Futures is the second major prop trading product launched under the Blueberry Markets brand, following the success of Blueberry Funded, their CFD-based prop firm. The setup is intentionally simple — you have two one-step challenges, identical targets across all account sizes, and a choice between the more forgiving Ascent (end-of-day drawdown) or the tighter Accelerated (real-time trailing drawdown). Fast execution through BlackArrow and a focused CME market list keep the trading conditions clean and predictable, giving you a user-friendly and straightforward path to funded trading without unnecessary complexity.
pros: Broker backed by ASIC regulated Blueberry Markets
Simple one-step challenges with clear rules
No commissions or hidden trading fees
Strong CME market selection with deep liquidity
cons: Payout caps and buffer reduce flexibility
bestFor: Futures traders who value clear rules, fast execution, and strong broker backing from an ASIC-regulated parent company
notIdealFor: Grid traders, heavy martingale users, and anyone who depends on automation, overnight swing holding, or needs multiple platform options

categorySections.challenges: Blueberry Futures offers two one-step challenges — **Ascent** (EOD drawdown) and **Accelerated** (trailing drawdown) — across $50K, $100K, and $150K account sizes. Both share the same profit targets ($3,000, $6,000, $10,000) and 30-day completion window. Ascent requires 2 active days with $200+ profit, while Accelerated needs just 1 day. Fees start at **$184** (Accelerated) and **$245** (Ascent). No daily loss limit — only the global drawdown matters. Contract caps scale with account size: 2/6/9 minis or 20/60/90 micros. Blueberry earned **9/10** for challenges.
categorySections.spreads: Blueberry Futures uses a **commission-free** structure where all execution costs are built into the BlackArrow environment. No per-contract platform fees, routing charges, or exchange costs during evaluations or funded accounts. The one-off challenge fee covers full access to BlackArrow, real-time Level 1 CME data, and all supported markets. Optional costs include the **$61/month** professional DOM feed and a monthly subscription for premium analytics and 15% reset discounts. Reset fees range from **$79–$310** depending on account type and size. Blueberry earned **9/10** for pricing.
categorySections.markets: Blueberry Futures provides a focused set of **CME futures** covering index futures (ES, NQ, RTY, EMD), micro equities (MES, MNQ, M2K), forex futures (6E, 6B, 6A, 6C, 6J, 6S, 6N), livestock (HE, LE, GF), and micro crypto (MBT, MET). All instruments trade on CME, CBOT, NYMEX, or COMEX with standard tick values and contract caps per account size. The absence of crypto, stocks, ETFs, or energy futures trims a point. Blueberry earned **9/10** for markets.
categorySections.platforms: Blueberry Futures uses **BlackArrow** as its sole trading platform across evaluations and funded accounts. The platform is purpose-built for futures with fast execution, DOM support, and chart tools. Desktop offers the most complete setup, web trader is reliable for routine management, and the mobile app suits monitoring. No MT4/MT5/TradingView integration. Manual execution required under Blueberry rules — no automation hooks or copy trading features. Blueberry earned **7.5/10** for platforms.
categorySections.payouts: Profit splits reach up to **90%**. Payout cycles require five profitable trading days with at least $200 net profit each. Withdrawals range from **$500 to $2,500** per cycle. A $2,100 account buffer must be maintained after each payout. Blueberry processes withdrawals through **RiseWorks** (bank transfers) and **USDT on TRC-20**. KYC verification through Veriff is required before the first withdrawal. Blueberry earned **8/10** for payouts.
categorySections.support: Blueberry Futures has 7-day customer support availability with ticketing and chat. The Discord server provides announcements, promotions, and community discussion. Educational tools sit inside the dashboard — the monthly subscription provides extended analytics, heatmaps, and journaling features. No formal course or training library is available. Blueberry earned **7/10** for support.
categorySections.trust: Blueberry Futures is backed by **Blueberry Markets**, an **ASIC-regulated** CFD brokerage, and supported by the established **Blueberry Funded** prop firm. The wider ecosystem includes over 15,000 active traders and $3.6 million in total payouts. Country restrictions cover Australia, Russia, Belarus, Iran, Myanmar, Sudan, Zimbabwe, and sanctioned Ukrainian regions. As a newly launched futures product, it does not yet have Trustpilot history but benefits heavily from its parent brand infrastructure. Blueberry earned **9.5/10** for trust.

verdictBlocks.heading: Blueberry Futures Final Score
verdictBlocks.body:
Blueberry Futures lands at 85/100 overall, helped by strong challenges, tight pricing, deep CME markets, and one of the best trust profiles in the futures prop space. It suits traders who already understand futures behaviour and want a clean, rules-based trading environment backed by a regulated brokerage, rather than a flashy start-up. Ascent is a good fit if you like EOD-style risk with room to hold through noise, while Accelerated appeals more to disciplined intraday traders who are comfortable protecting equity highs.

It will not suit everyone — grid traders, heavy martingale users, and anyone who depends on automation or overnight swing holding will likely find the rules too restrictive. If you want the broadest platform choice, or you rely heavily on built-in news and research, you may prefer a different setup like FXIFY Futures or FundedNext Futures.

But for futures traders who value clear rules, consistently fast execution, and strong CFD broker backing, it's one of the best futures prop firms.

faq.a: Yes, Blueberry is considered a trusted brand because it is a broker-backed prop firm, connected to Blueberry Markets, a regulated CFD brokerage, and supported by the established Blueberry Funded prop firm. While Blueberry Futures itself is new and still building independent reviews, the wider ecosystem provides a stable operational base, treasury management, and verified payout history.

Yes, Blueberry Futures is effectively commission free because all execution costs are already built into the BlackArrow environment. You do not pay separate routing fees, platform charges, or per-contract commissions during evaluations or funded accounts. You only pay the initial challenge fee, optional add-ons, or a reset if you breach the drawdown.

Yes, prop trading is high risk because strict drawdown limits mean any breach ends the account immediately. In futures, that risk feels sharper due to fast price movement and contract volatility. The difference compared to trading your own capital is that your financial risk is capped at the initial challenge fee and any resets, rather than losses coming directly from your personal funds.
